skill.name: database-design
skill.description: Guides practical database design decisions, including database selection, schema modeling, indexes, migrations, ORM choice, and query optimization.
skill.summary: Plan database schemas, indexes, migrations, and ORM choices from project context.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found markdown code fences, backticked filenames, and database terminology rather than executable shell code or crypto use. No prompt injection, network access, credential handling, or data exfiltration was found. The included validator script performs local filesystem discovery and reads schema files under a user-selected project path, so the residual risk is low and expected for this skill.

low_findings.title: Local Project File Scanning by Validator Script
low_findings.description: The optional validator accepts a project path, recursively finds Prisma and Drizzle schema files, and reads matching Prisma schema content. This is expected for a schema validation helper and is limited to local files selected by the user, with no network transfer or command execution found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly uses glob and read_text on a user-provided project path. The behavior is legitimate for validation and lacks network or shell execution, so the risk is low.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown code fences, backticked file names, or documentation tables. No shell invocation, subprocess call, eval, or dynamic command construction was found at the reported locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain documentation diagrams and file names, not executable code. There is no syntax or runtime context for command execution in these markdown files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography indicators correspond to database and documentation terms such as Hash index, UUID, and HNSW. No hashing algorithm implementation, password handling, or cryptographic decision point was found in the reported files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found database design terminology and metadata rather than cryptographic code. The locations do not contain algorithm selection or secret processing.

content.value_statement: Database decisions often fail when teams copy defaults without checking deployment, query, and scaling needs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical database, schema, index, ORM, migration, and optimization choices.
actual_capabilities: Compares PostgreSQL, Neon, Turso, SQLite, PlanetScale, and CockroachDB for common project contexts.
Guides schema design choices for normalization, primary keys, timestamps, relationships, and delete behavior.
Explains index selection, including B-tree, hash, GIN, GiST, and vector index use cases.
Helps choose between Drizzle, Prisma, Kysely, raw SQL, and SQLAlchemy based on deployment needs.
Provides migration guidance for nullable additions, backfills, concurrent indexes, renames, and rollback planning.
Includes a local Prisma schema validator for naming, identifiers, timestamps, and foreign key index suggestions.
limitations: It provides guidance and validation suggestions, not a complete database architecture review.
The included validator focuses on Prisma and only stubs Drizzle validation.
It does not run migrations, connect to databases, or benchmark live queries.
Recommendations may need adjustment for strict compliance, legacy systems, or unusual database engines.

use_cases.title: Choose a Database for a New App
use_cases.description: Compare database options against deployment model, query complexity, edge needs, vector search, and global distribution requirements.

use_cases.title: Review a Product Schema Before Build
use_cases.description: Check entities, relationships, primary keys, timestamps, foreign keys, and normalization choices before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Improve Slow Queries and Migrations
use_cases.description: Use index, N+1, EXPLAIN ANALYZE, and migration principles to reduce production risk and improve performance.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a Database
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a database for this app. Ask for missing context about deployment, query complexity, edge needs, vector search, and global distribution before recommending options.

prompt_templates.title: Design Core Tables
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a database schema for these requirements. Explain normalization choices, primary keys, timestamps, relationships, foreign keys, and indexes in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Review ORM Choice
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Drizzle, Prisma, Kysely, raw SQL, and SQLAlchemy for this project. Consider deployment target, bundle size, migrations, developer experience, and query control.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Safe Migration and Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this proposed schema change and query workload. Create a zero-downtime migration plan, index strategy, N+1 prevention plan, and rollback checklist.

output_examples.input: A serverless SaaS app needs relational data, branching for previews, and moderate analytical queries.
output_examples.output: The skill would compare Neon and Supabase as PostgreSQL options, explain serverless trade-offs, and recommend indexes and migration safety checks.

output_examples.input: A team has slow user dashboard queries with repeated child lookups.
output_examples.output: The skill would identify a likely N+1 pattern, suggest eager loading or joins, recommend EXPLAIN ANALYZE, and review missing foreign key indexes.

output_examples.input: A Prisma schema is missing timestamps and foreign key indexes.
output_examples.output: The skill would flag missing createdAt fields, recommend foreign key indexes, and explain which changes should be migrated in safe phases.

best_practices: Start by clarifying deployment, query complexity, latency, distribution, and data growth before choosing a database.
Design relationships and indexes from real query patterns, not from table names alone.
Plan migrations as staged production changes with backfills, rollback steps, and tests on copied data.
anti_patterns: Defaulting to PostgreSQL, Prisma, or any single tool before checking project constraints.
Adding indexes to every column without measuring query patterns and write costs.
Combining breaking schema changes, data backfills, and application deploys in one step.

faq.question: Can this skill choose between PostgreSQL and SQLite?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares database options using deployment model, relational needs, edge requirements, vector search, and distribution needs.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready SQL migrations?
faq.answer: No. It provides migration strategy guidance, but engineers should write, test, and review final migrations.

faq.question: Can it validate a Prisma schema?
faq.answer: Yes. The included script checks Prisma schemas for naming, identifiers, timestamps, and foreign key index suggestions.

faq.question: Does it support Drizzle projects?
faq.answer: Partially. It documents Drizzle selection guidance, but the validator currently finds Drizzle files without deep validation.

faq.question: Is this useful for query performance work?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers N+1 issues, EXPLAIN ANALYZE review, missing indexes, selected columns, joins, pagination, and caching decisions.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this in an existing repository?
faq.answer: Yes. They can use the guidance files and optional local validator to review schema design and database decisions.
